back_translation: For the choirmaster. With stringed instruments, according to Sheminith. A psalm of David.
O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger, nor discipline me in Your fierce wrath.
O LORD, be merciful to me, for I am languishing; O LORD, heal me, for my bones tremble.
My soul is greatly distressed. O LORD, how long?
O LORD, turn back and deliver my life; save me for the sake of Your steadfast love.
For in death no one remembers You; in the realm of the dead, who will praise You?
I am weary with groaning; every night I weep until my bed is soaked; my tears drench my couch.
My eye wastes away from grief, and grows weak because of all my foes.
Depart from me, all you who do evil, for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
The LORD has heard my plea; the LORD accepts my prayer.
All my enemies will be ashamed and greatly dismayed; they will turn back and be put to shame in an instant.
